gamerule.allowSuicide.description:
Allow players to kill themselves at any time with /suicide (no permissions required).
Items are still lost if gamerule keepInventory is false.
gamerule.allowFindingLastDeath.descripti: Allow using /finddeath to get coordinates of last death (no permissions required).
gamerule.allowWarpingToLastDeath.descrip: Allow using /warpdeath to teleport to your last death (no permissions required).
gamerule.deathTeleportCooldown.descripti: Time in ticks (20 = 1 second) after using /warpdeath before the player can use it again, regardless of any deaths after using the command.
gamerule.allowMultipleWarpsPerDeath.desc: Allow players to warp multiple times to the same death. It's good to disable this if you don't trust the integrity of players, as enabling this opens the opportunity of simply dying once at home, and from then on using /warpdeath to go home.
gamerule.deathWarpExpireTime.description: Time in ticks (20 = 1 second) after dying before you lose the opportunity to use /warpdeath. Use to ensure players can't just die at home, and then "save up" the /warpdeath for later to return home while somewhere like The Nether.
commands.resetminecrafttoolsconfig.succe: Config Reset for Minecraft Tools mod (MODID: "minecraft_tools")
commands.suicide.failure: %s failed suicide because gamerule allowSuicide is false.
commands.suicide.failure.notentity: %s failed suicide because /suicide must be used on an entity.
commands.finddeath.success: %s last died at coordinates [%s, %s, %s] in dimension %s
commands.finddeath.failure.neverdied: %s did not have a last death to find.
commands.finddeath.failure.notplayer: /finddeath must be used on a Player (%s was not a Player)!
commands.finddeath.failure.disabled: %s failed to find last death because gamerule allowFindingLastDeath is false.
commands.warpdeath.success: %s teleported to last death ([%s, %s, %s] in dimension %s).
commands.warpdeath.failure.neverdied: %s did not have a last death to teleport to.
commands.warpdeath.failure.notplayer: /warpdeath must be used on a Player (%s was not a Player)!
commands.warpdeath.failure.disabled: %s failed to teleport to last death because gamerule allowWarpingToLastDeath is false.
commands.warpdeath.failure.notagain: %s failed to teleport to last death (gamerule allowMultipleWarpsPerDeath is false, and they have already used /warpdeath to warp to that death point).
commands.warpdeath.failure.toolate: %s failed to teleport to last death because it has been %s second(s) (%s tick(s)) since they died, and gamerule deathWarpExpireTime is %s second(s) (%s tick(s)).
commands.warpdeath.failure.tooearly: %s failed to teleport to last death because it has only been %s second(s) (%s tick(s)) since they last used /warpdeath, and gamerule deathTeleportCooldown is %s second(s) (%s tick(s)).
commands.itemkill.success.none: There were no dropped items to kill.
commands.itemkill.failure.nonplayerandem: Must provide either target selector or be executed by a Player.
commands.xpkill.success.none: There were no Experience Orbs to kill.
commands.dropkill.success.none: There were no mobloot drops to kill.
commands.xpkill.failure.nonplayerandempt: Must provide either target selector or be executed by a Player.
----COMMENT----: Can't seem to find anything about command descriptions for /help so I'll just leave this as it is below (doesn't work yet)
commands.itemkill.description: Kills all entities in target selector passed that are dropped items (so "/itemkill @e" would just be "/kill @e[type=item]"), defaults to @e if no argument is passed. Must be executed by a player if no argument is given.
